entities.name: RICE
entities.description: The primary cereal crop discussed in the context of lowland and aerobic systems

entities.name: ASIA
entities.description: The geographical region where lowland rice systems are major contributors to global supply

entities.name: LOWLAND RICE SYSTEM
entities.description: A sustainable agricultural system where rice is grown in sequence under submerged conditions

entities.name: SUBMERGED CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environmental state where soil is saturated with water, typical for lowland rice

entities.name: WATER SHORTAGES
entities.description: The lack of sufficient water resources for irrigation in certain regions

entities.name: MOISTURE REGIMES
entities.description: Different levels of soil water availability used to optimize water use and productivity

entities.name: FLOODED CONDITIONS
entities.description: A state of soil saturation used in traditional rice cultivation

entities.name: AEROBIC CONDITIONS
entities.description: Non-flooded soil conditions used for rice cultivation to save water

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY
entities.description: The ability of the soil to sustain plant growth by providing essential nutrients

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The process of analyzing and adjusting soil nutrients to maintain productivity

entities.name: CHEMICAL FERTILITY
entities.description: The chemical properties and nutrient levels of the soil

entities.name: ORGANIC MATTER
entities.description: The organic component of soil derived from decomposed plant and animal materials

entities.name: MICRONUTRIENTS
entities.description: Essential trace elements required by rice for growth

entities.name: SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: The long-term viability and maintenance of the agricultural system

entities.name: CROPPING SYSTEM
entities.description: The pattern of crops grown in a specific area over a period of time

entities.name: CHEMICAL AND BIOLOGICAL CONSTRAINTS
entities.description: Site-specific factors that limit the sustainability of continuous aerobic rice
